text:
In a forest clearing under a whispering moon, Oliver found comfort in the simple things. He'd sit under an ancient oak, watching feathers dance on silk gusts.

One night, a soft rustle drew his attention. Curious, Oliver got up. The mist shifted like velvet. Shadows played near a hidden pond.

A mouse glided near him. He petted it gently. It squeaked happily.

That night strange stars winked silent smiles. Oliver dreamed of flying mice.

He woke with a laugh. The woods felt alive. Maybe mice knew magic after all.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
